help.title: Help
help.feature.openFiles: Open Markdown files via dialog (Ctrl+O), drag-and-drop, “Open with” in Explorer, or the recent-files list.
help.feature.tabs: Multiple files in tabs side-by-side; up to two columns — tabs can be moved between columns by drag-and-drop or right-click menu.
help.feature.viewModes: Three views per tab: Source, Split, and Rendered. The chosen mode is remembered per file.
help.feature.sourceToggles: Optional word-wrap and line numbers in the source view — togglable per tab.
help.feature.foldGutter: Heading and list/block folding in the source view with hierarchy tracks on the left; togglable per tab.
help.feature.outline: Table-of-contents sidebar per column with a clickable heading tree, jump-on-click and folding sync.
help.feature.backlinks: Backlinks sidebar per column: shows incoming wiki and Markdown links to the active file from its folder and up to two sub-folder levels, with live updates on save.
help.feature.anchorLinks: In-document anchor links like [Text](#section) work in the render pane.
help.feature.search: Full-text search (Ctrl+F) with live matches, regular expressions (.* toggle) and case sensitivity (Aa). The question mark in the search bar shows a regex quick reference.
help.feature.autoReload: Changed files are reloaded automatically; deleted files are marked with a strikethrough in the tab.
help.feature.restoreSession: Session (tabs, columns, views) is saved on exit and restored on next launch — optional via the Help menu.
help.feature.links: Markdown links to other .md files open them in a new tab; http(s) and mailto links open in the system default; wiki-style links like [[Target]] are supported.
help.feature.theme: Light and dark theme: choose manually via View → Theme or via the sun/moon/system icon in the status bar. The default follows the Windows system theme.
help.feature.languages: Interface in German, English, French, Spanish, and Italian — switch via the language selector in the status bar.
help.feature.windowState: Window position, size, and maximized state are remembered on exit and restored on next launch.
help.feature.multiWindow: Move tabs to a new or existing window: right-click a tab and choose “Move to” or “Copy to”; with multiple windows open, a submenu lists all other windows as targets. The window title shows the suffix “(Window N)” when multiple windows are open, so source and target windows are unambiguous. Multiple windows can be placed on different monitors; with session restore enabled, all open windows are reopened on the next launch.
help.feature.editMode: Edit Markdown content in the source pane (pencil in the status bar or Ctrl+E). Edit mode is only available in source or split view; clicking the pencil in render view automatically switches to split and activates the editor.
help.feature.save: Save (Ctrl+S) and Save As (Ctrl+Shift+S). Unsaved changes are marked with • in the tab and window title; closing a modified tab shows a confirmation dialog. On external change with a dirty buffer, the app asks whether to reload or keep the local version.
help.feature.autoSave: Optional: auto-save (File → Auto Save). Saves 2 seconds after the last edit or on window focus loss. Tabs without a path (“Untitled”) are not auto-saved.
help.feature.exportPortable: Use File → Export → Portable Markdown… to save the current file as portable Markdown. SCG tables are replaced with inline HTML tables, so the file also renders as a real table in third-party Markdown renderers (GitHub, VS Code). Details in the 'SCG Table' help tab.
help.feature.searchReplace: Search and Replace (Ctrl+H) in edit mode, with regex backreferences ($1, $2, …) in the replacement text. “Replace All” is a single transaction — Ctrl+Z undoes it as a whole.
help.feature.menuBar: Native menu bar with File, View, and Help menus. Alt activates keyboard control; mnemonics (e.g. Alt+F for File) jump directly to the menu.
help.feature.newTab: File → New (Ctrl+N) opens an empty “Untitled” tab in the active window, in edit mode with split view. The first save opens Save As.
help.shortcut.moveTab: Move active tab to the other column
help.shortcut.searchNavEnter: Next / previous match (in the search bar)
help.shortcut.toggleOutline: Show/hide table-of-contents sidebar in the active column
help.shortcut.toggleBacklinks: Show/hide backlinks sidebar in the active column
help.shortcut.foldRegion: Fold heading or block region at cursor
help.feature.tabIndent: Tab and Shift+Tab indent or outdent Markdown list items by one level. Ordered lists restart at 1. on indent; unordered and task lists keep their marker. Outside of lists the standard Tab behaviour is unchanged.
help.feature.zoom: Zoom the content of each tab independently: Ctrl + + / Ctrl + - in 10 % steps, Ctrl + 0 resets to 100 %, also via Ctrl + Mouse wheel. The status bar shows the current zoom factor when it differs from 100 %; click it to reset. Zoom is volatile and does not survive closing the window.
help.feature.settings: Settings dialog (File → Settings or Ctrl + ,) lets you configure font family and size for the editor and render pane separately. Values are persistent and apply to all open windows; live preview in the dialog, OK / Apply / Cancel.
help.feature.focusMode: Focus mode (Ctrl + Shift + F or View → Focus Mode) hides the tab bar, status bar and sidebars for distraction-free writing. The menu bar remains reachable via Alt. Esc exits focus mode unless a dialog or menu takes precedence.
help.feature.typewriterScroll: Typewriter scroll (View → Typewriter Scroll) keeps the cursor line vertically centred in the editor as soon as the cursor moves. Active only in edit mode.
help.feature.codeHighlight: Code blocks with a language tag (e.g. ```javascript) get syntax highlighting in the render pane. The colour palette follows the system theme.
help.feature.katex: Math is typeset with KaTeX: $…$ inline, $$…$$ as a block. Dollar amounts in running text stay untouched.
help.feature.mermaid: Code blocks with the mermaid language tag render as SVG diagrams in the render pane (flowchart, sequence, gantt, class and more). Diagrams follow the theme.
help.feature.linter: Markdown light linter discreetly marks four common issues in the editor: bare URLs (without Markdown link syntax), empty link text, missing alt text and wiki links whose target is not found in the search scope. Hover for an explanation.
help.feature.scgTable: Tables with multi-line block cells via a scg-table code block, MediaWiki-like syntax. Details and examples in the 'SCG Table' help tab.
help.feature.scgTableExtended: SCG tables additionally support sortable headers (`{|+sortable`), status highlighting (`|.error`, `|-.warn` etc.) and column-default alignment (`{|+cols="left right right"`). Details and examples in the 'SCG Table' help tab.
help.feature.updateCheck: Update detection: the app checks for new versions in the background (45 seconds after startup, then every 24 hours) and manually via Help → Check for Updates. When a new version is found, a dialog opens with a link to the GitHub release page. Automatic download and installation will come with a signed setup EXE in a future release.
help.shortcut.zoom: Zoom in, zoom out, reset to 100 %
help.shortcut.tabIndent: Indent / outdent Markdown list item by one level

linter.bareUrl.tooltip: URL without Markdown link syntax. Prefer [label](URL) or autolink <URL>.
